rows.description: Soluta Varia
rows.notes: Consensus on header description and no amounts. Gemini-flash description slightly closer to image (no period at the end).

rows.description: Jany. 7. allowed q. Lumberd Lond Tax for 2 years Rent
rows.notes: Chose gemini-flash's description and amounts. Visual inspection confirms '19' for shillings (gpt-5-mini had 17) and 'Lond Tax' for description. 'allowed' and 'q.' also seem better from gemini-flash.
rows.english_description: Payment to Lumberd for London tax relating to two years' rent.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Lumberd for London tax relating to two years' rent, amounting to £1 19s 4d.

rows.description: July 14. P.d. Candlenes come Price
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Description from gemini-flash 'Candlenes' is more plausible than gpt-5-mini's 'Conduins', though the text is slightly cramped. gpt-5-mini's note acknowledging ambiguity is better quality than gemini-flash's 'clear'.
rows.english_description: Payment for candles (household provision).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for candles (household provision), amounting to £0 1s 0d.

rows.description: Mar.h 5. P.d. Porter for lighting Lamps y. printer
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms 'Mar.h 5.' and 'Lamps y. printer' from gemini-flash's description is correct. gpt-5-mini had 'March 6.' and 'Comp ye winter', which is visually incorrect.
rows.english_description: Payment to the porter for lighting the lamps.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the porter for lighting the lamps, amounting to £1 0s 0d.

rows.description: 7. allowed for taking out Copy of a Letter of Attorney for y.
rows.notes: Following gemini-flash's segmentation as it accurately reflects the visual separation of description lines in the ledger. This line is descriptive text for the subsequent entry and has no associated amount on its own line. gpt-5-mini merged this description with the next line.
rows.english_description: Expense for obtaining a copy of a letter of attorney.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Expense for obtaining a copy of a letter of attorney; no amount recorded.

rows.description: Exchequer Annuity
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms this description and amounts for this line. Aligned with gemini-flash's segmentation, where the amount '00 5 0' is clearly next to 'Exchequer Annuity'. gpt-5-mini merged this description with the previous line.
rows.english_description: Payment of an exchequer annuity.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of an exchequer annuity, amounting to £0 5s 0d.

rows.description: 20. P.d. Horner Bill of Expencey ab.t y.e Lead stealers
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Description from gemini-flash is visually more accurate regarding 'Expencey', 'ab.t', 'y.e', and 'Lead stealers' compared to gpt-5-mini's transcription.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Horner for expenses connected with stolen lead.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Horner for expenses connected with stolen lead, amounting to £1 10s 2d.

rows.description: 1751 June 8. P.d. Mr Rowlins Stamp & engrossing Letter, Attorney &c.
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. For description, 'engrossing' from gpt-5-mini is a better contextual fit (legal term) and visually plausible than gemini-flash's 'suprosing'. 'P.d.', 'Rowlins' (name spelling), and capital 'Stamp' (capitalization) from gemini-flash seem visually better.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Rowlins for stamp and engrossing of a letter of attorney and related legal work.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Rowlins for stamp and engrossing of a letter of attorney and related legal work, amounting to £0 10s 6d.

rows.description: 27. P.d. Basser W.M. shron Auger One Sum Cost.
rows.notes: Visual inspection for amounts indicates '5' pounds is clear, while a trailing '0' (for '50') is very faint and possibly non-existent; choosing '5' (gpt-5-mini) over '50' (gemini-flash). For description, 'P.d. Basser' (gemini-flash) is more plausible than 'Pd Sumner' (gpt-5-mini) for the initial words. The rest of gemini-flash's description ('W.M. shron Auger One Sum Cost') is a plausible attempt at difficult text, as acknowledged by gpt-5-mini's 'illegible' note.
rows.english_description: Lump‑sum payment for an auger (cost recorded as a single sum).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Lump‑sum payment for an auger, amounting to £5 0s 0d.

rows.description: July 9. P.d. L. Dey Com Price
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's description ('P.d. L. Dey') is more accurate than gpt-5-mini's ('Pd L: Day').
rows.english_description: Payment to L. Dey for common (provisions) price.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to L. Dey for common provisions price, amounting to £0 1s 0d.

rows.description: — 12. P.d. Mr Lawrense &c for Candles on acct.
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts, which are visually clear. 'Lawrense' (gemini-flash) is visually more accurate than 'Lawrence' (gpt-5-mini). gpt-5-mini's note about the amount being large for 'candles' is a good observation but the numbers are clearly '30'.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Lawrence on account for candles.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Lawrence on account for candles, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.description: — 25. P.d. Mr Mollineuxs Bill home to Oct.r 12. 1750.
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's description, particularly the date '25.', the name 'Mollineuxs', and 'home to Oct.r 12. 1750.' gpt-5-mini had errors in these details ('23.', 'Mollinux', 'from 12 Octr 1750').
rows.english_description: Payment of Mr Mollineux's bill up to 12 October 1750.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Mr Mollineux's bill up to 12 October 1750, amounting to £14 19s 0d.

rows.description: — P.d. Cook for acct. Dinner & X.mas goodes.
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's description, including the leading dash '— P.d.' and 'X.mas goodes'. gpt-5-mini incorrectly dated it '23.' and marked 'X.mas goodes' as indistinct, which is visually legible.
rows.english_description: Payment to the cook for a dinner account and Christmas provisions.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the cook for a dinner account and Christmas provisions, amounting to £15 11s 6d.

rows.description: Oct.r 10. P.d. Mich: Com Price
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual confirmation of gemini-flash's description, particularly 'Oct.r' and 'P.d.', which are more accurate than gpt-5-mini's 'Octr' and 'Pd'.
rows.english_description: Payment for common/provisions price (Michael-related entry).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for common/provisions price, amounting to £0 1s 0d.

rows.description: — P.d. a Bill Due April 30th Last for 2 Batty Books
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's description, particularly the leading '— P.d. a' and 'Batty' (gpt-5-mini had 'A' and 'Battry').
rows.english_description: Payment of a bill that was due on 30 April for two Battel (account) books.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of a bill that was due on 30 April for two battel books, amounting to £1 4s 0d.

rows.description: — 24. P.d. Smith & Hachworth & Bill for Feb.y 1749. in all
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's amounts, especially '4' pence (gpt-5-mini had '1' pence), and the full description. gpt-5-mini also had several inaccuracies in names and dates ('Hackworth & Mills per Decr.').
rows.english_description: Payment of Smith & Hachworth's bill for February 1749 (total paid on this date).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Smith & Hachworth's bill for February 1749, amounting to £10 7s 4½d.

rows.description: — P.d. Cookes Bill for Mess.rs Broom, &c.
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's description, particularly '— P.d. Cookes' and 'Mess.rs Broom'. gpt-5-mini misread the names as 'Pd Cooke Bill for Mary Brown'.
rows.english_description: Payment of the cook's bill for Messrs Broom and others.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of the cook's bill for Messrs Broom and others, amounting to £3 13s 4d.

rows.description: — 26. P.d. Horner for Bathel Books, paper, Ink, &c.
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual check confirms '— 26. P.d.'. For 'Bathel' (gemini-flash) vs 'Battel' (gpt-5-mini), 'Bathel' seems slightly more accurate visually, though 'Battel' is also a plausible term. gpt-5-mini's note on ambiguity is good.
rows.english_description: Payment to Horner for battel/account books, paper, ink, and similar stationery.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Horner for battel/account books, paper, and ink, amounting to £0 17s 7d.

rows.description: — 28. allowed Mr Upton Library Book,
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's description, particularly the leading dash, 'allowed' and 'Book,' (singular with comma), which gpt-5-mini had as 'allowd' and 'Books'.
rows.english_description: Payment approved for Mr Upton for a library book.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment approved for Mr Upton for a library book, amounting to £8 12s 0d.

rows.description: — allowed Mr Upton Proper Expences
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual check confirms leading dash '—' and 'allowed'. For the ambiguous word, 'Proper' (gpt-5-mini) is more convincing than 'Profery' (gemini-flash) based on typical letter forms and contextual meaning. Both models correctly identified the difficulty of this word.
rows.english_description: Allowance to Mr Upton for his proper expenses (reimbursement).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Allowance to Mr Upton for his proper expenses, amounting to £7 16s 1d.

rows.description: — 30. P.d. Gearler Flatten Bill
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms '8' pounds (gpt-5-mini had 6) and '— 30. P.d.' for gemini-flash. While the names 'Gearler Flatten' are faint, gemini-flash's reading seems more accurate than gpt-5-mini's 'Tearcher Slattern' for the letter forms present.
rows.english_description: Payment of Gearler Flatten's bill (purpose not clearly stated).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Gearler Flatten's bill, amounting to £8 11s 0d.

rows.description: — P.d. Broyle Seavenger
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms amounts '0 2 6' as extracted by gpt-5-mini (gemini-flash had 2 0 6). For description, the leading '— P.d.' (gemini-flash) is present. 'Broyle' (gemini-flash) seems more accurate than 'Tayl'r' (gpt-5-mini), and 'Seavenger' (gemini-flash) has a capital S.
rows.english_description: Payment to Broyle (a scavenger or official) — purpose unclear but appears an official fee.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Broyle (scavenger/official fee), amounting to £0 2s 6d.

rows.description: — 31. P.d. Mollineux Bill for Pewter &c. how to y.e Date
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's description as accurate, including '— P.d. Mollineux', 'Pewter', and 'how to y.e Date'. gpt-5-mini had several inaccuracies ('Mollinex', 'Pewster') and marked parts as indistinct that are legible.
rows.english_description: Payment of Mr Mollineux's bill for pewter and related items up to the date.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Mr Mollineux's bill for pewter and related items up to the date, amounting to £14 5s 0d.

rows.description: — allowed Wm Quies Lond Tax for year 1750.
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's description, particularly '— allowed Wm Quies Lond Tax'. gpt-5-mini misread 'Quies' as 'Dunes' and 'Lond Tax' as 'Land tax'.
rows.english_description: Payment to William Quies for London tax for the year 1750.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to William Quies for London tax for the year 1750, amounting to £0 18s 8d.

rows.description: — gave at Mr Brooklys Disposall for Servants
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's description, particularly '— gave at Mr Brooklys Disposall for Servants'. gpt-5-mini had multiple inaccuracies ('Franklyns(?) Project').
rows.english_description: Sum given at Mr Brooklyn's disposal for the college servants.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Sum given at Mr Brooklyn's disposal for the college servants, amounting to £0 2s 6d.

rows.notes: Consensus on amounts and row type. Visual inspection confirms no descriptive text on this total line, so gemini-flash's empty description is more accurate than gpt-5-mini's 'Total (line beneath entries)'.

rows.description: Nov.r 2. 1751. - Prodicus Computus examinatus & approbatus per Property (viz. Deb: Thesansro a D. Rectore -
rows.notes: Consensus on amounts, which are visually confirmed including the faint '1' in pounds. For description, combined best parts from both models: 'Nov.r', 'Prodicus' (gemini-flash), 'examinatus & approbatus', 'Property' (gpt-5-mini), and 'Thesansro a D. Rectore -' (gemini-flash). Both models provided good notes on the legibility challenges.
rows.english_description: Computed account examined and approved by the governing officers on 2 November 1751 (certification of the account).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Computed account examined and approved by the governing officers on 2 November 1751, amounting to £168 10s 0½d.

rows.description: Franciscum Webber Rectorem.
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms description and 'entry' type for this signature line. gemini-flash's transcription is more accurate on capitalization ('Franciscum') and spelling ('Rectorem.') than gpt-5-mini.
rows.english_description: Signature of Francis Webber in his capacity as Rector.
rows.english_description_with_amount: No amount recorded with this signature.

rows.description: Thomam Bray Sub rectorem.
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms description and 'entry' type for this signature line. gemini-flash is more accurate on 'Thomam' (gpt-5-mini had 'Thomas') and the spacing 'Sub rectorem.' (gpt-5-mini had 'Subrectorum').
rows.english_description: Signature of Thomas Bray in his capacity as sub‑rector.
rows.english_description_with_amount: No amount recorded with this signature.

rows.description: Franciscum Upton Sacarum.
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms description and 'entry' type for this signature line. Corrected 'francisum' (gemini-flash) to 'Franciscum' (capital F from image). 'Sacarum.' is clear, unlike gpt-5-mini's 'word unclear'.
rows.english_description: Signature of Francis Upton in his ecclesiastical/sacristan role.
rows.english_description_with_amount: No amount recorded with this signature.

rows.description: Jacobym Coporat. J.P.P.
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms description and 'entry' type for this signature line. gemini-flash's reading of 'Jacobym Coporat. J.P.P.' is much more accurate than gpt-5-mini's 'Jacobus Colvard (or Colvardus) J.M.P.'.
rows.english_description: Payment made to Jacobus Coporat, a named payee (miscellaneous payment).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment made to Jacobus Coporat, a named payee; amount not recorded.

rows.description: Jac. Pottertur & F.P. Prob.e com
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms 'entry' type. gemini-flash's transcription of 'Jac. Pottertur & F.P. Prob.e com' is significantly more accurate than gpt-5-mini's 'J H Portater (illegible) & P. Robt.', despite the challenging script. gpt-5-mini correctly noted difficulty, but gemini-flash provided a better reading.
rows.english_description: Payment to Jacobus Potter and F. P., apparently for probate/commission fees or similar miscellaneous charges.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Jacobus Potter and F. P., apparently for probate/commission fees; amount not recorded.

rows.description: Johannem Jowell
rows.notes: Visual verification confirms description and 'entry' type for this signature line. 'Jowell' (gemini-flash) is clearly more accurate than 'Townell' (gpt-5-mini) for the surname.
rows.english_description: Payment made to John Jowell, a named payee.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment made to John Jowell, a named payee; amount not recorded.
